---
title: "Lecture 10 Quiz: CSS Box Model & Positioning"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Lectures](/lectures/lecture-10.qmd) | [Week 3 Content](/content/week-03.qmd) | [Lab 3](/labs/lab-03.qmd) | [Assignment 3](/assignment/03-assignment.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 10 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. content, border, padding, margin", "B. margin, border, padding, content", "C. padding, margin, border, content", "D. border, margin, content, padding"]
question = "Question 1: What is the correct outside-to-inside order in the CSS box model?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. position: static", "B. position: relative", "C. position: fixed", "D. position: sticky"]
question = "Question 2: Which position value keeps an element attached to the viewport while scrolling?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Space outside border", "B. Space inside border", "C. Border thickness", "D. Content alignment"]
question = "Question 3: Padding controls which part of spacing?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. width includes content only", "B. width includes margin only", "C. width includes padding and border", "D. width is ignored"]
question = "Question 4: With `box-sizing: border-box`, the declared width means:"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. To set text style", "B. To control overlap/layer order", "C. To define border color", "D. To align items in flexbox"]
question = "Question 5: Why is `z-index` used in CSS positioning?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
